data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Rises to 4.254% This Week — Data Talk
data.description:
The 10-year yield rose 0.003 percentage point to 4.254% this week. The price fell 1/32 to 102 31/32. 




     --Largest one-week yield gain since Friday, March 7, 2025 



     --Snaps a two-week streak of falling yields 



     --Today it is down 0.116 percentage point and the price rose 30/32 price points 



     --Largest one-day yield decline since Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025 



     --Snaps a two-trading-day streak of rising yields 



     --Lowest yield since Friday, March 21, 2025 



     --Yield is off 0.548 percentage point from its 52-week high of 4.802% hit Monday, Jan. 13, 2025 



     --Yield is up 0.632 percentage point from its 52-week low of 3.622% hit Monday, Sept. 16, 2024 



     --Yield is up 0.062 percentage point from 52 weeks ago 



     --Yield is up 0.075 percentage point from its 2025 closing low of 4.178% hit Monday, March 3, 2025 



     --Month-to-date the yield is up 0.025 percentage point 



     --Year-to-date the yield is down 0.323 percentage point 





     Data based on 3 p.m. ET values 





     Source: Tradeweb FTSE U.S. Treasury Closing Prices 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 28, 2025 15:43 ET (19:43 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Dollar Tree is selling Family Dollar after a decade together. Here's why it didn't work.
data.description:
By Bill Peters

'It was just a culmination of things that all needed fixing,' analyst says

Roughly a decade ago, both Dollar Tree Inc. and archrival Dollar General Corp. were bidding for Family Dollar as the rapidly growing dollar-chain industry opened lots of new stores. When Dollar Tree (DLTR) finally closed on that bid in the summer of 2015, executives described the $8.5 billion deal as a "transformational opportunity" to sell more products in more places to more low-income consumers. There was talk of "cost synergies" and "store productivity."

But even with some big leadership changes and efforts to shovel cash into store upgrades, that vision, and the financials, never exactly materialized.

On Wednesday, Dollar Tree capitulated, announcing that it would sell Family Dollar to two private-equity firms for $1 billion, far less than it paid for the chain. And despite the synergy-related enthusiasm of the past decade, Dollar Tree Chief Executive Mike Creedon said Dollar Tree and Family Dollar were, in fact, "two different businesses with limited synergies" that were better off on their own.

Analysts cite a number of reasons for Family Dollar's difficulties, including low-income shoppers who are wrestling with inflation, competition with Dollar General (DG) and mass-market retailers like Walmart Inc. (WMT), and issues with logistics, too many store openings, poorly maintained stores and pricing.

"Customers noticed that, and it was just a culmination of things that all needed fixing," said Melius Research analyst Karen Short.

"You have to paint stores," she added later. "You have to change tiles. You have to change shelving. That's just day to day."

Leadership changes, store differences

Short noted that over the years, management made efforts to clean up Family Dollar stores, to bring in lower-priced items and to set up Family Dollar and Dollar Tree combo stores. It tried remodeling stores, selling more party supplies and alcohol, and adding freezer and cooler space.

But Family Dollar's sales, after seeing a pandemic-era lift, slipped last year, according to FactSet data, and were expected to fall further. Analysts remained concerned about profit margins. In 2023, Mike Witynski left his position as Dollar Tree's chief executive, a role taken up by former Dollar General Chief Executive Rick Dreiling, who brought with him hopes of a stronger turnaround but who left the chain in November due to health issues. Creedon replaced him.

Dollar Tree first said that it was reviewing "strategic alternatives" for Family Dollar last year, after announcing a year before that it planned to close about 970 Family Dollar stores. And analysts say that Dollar Tree and Family Dollar have notable differences - even though both have the word "dollar" in their names.

Dollar Tree sells a higher-end mix of discretionary items, like party supplies and greeting cards, which make up around half of its business, said John Zolidis, president of Quo Vadis Capital. The stores tend to target middle-income shoppers with locations in the suburbs, sometimes right next to a Walmart, and they are intended to be a bit more fun, and a bit more about the "treasure hunt" for bargains. Analysts have said Dollar Tree was the stronger business of the two.

Similar to Dollar General, Family Dollar mainly sells lots of low-priced consumables - like food, paper and plastics, household cleaners and health and beauty products - to lower-income consumers, he said. Family Dollar stores are often in urban or downtown areas or in rural areas where Walmart isn't, with the goal of drawing customers on a tight budget or who only need to pick up a few things.

But even as price increases and a higher cost of living over the past several years keep many consumers focused on the basics, Zolidis said those basics can be found in lots of other places. That has made it tough for Family Dollar to stand out.

"The consumables are something you can buy not just at dollar stores, but also at drugstores, big-box retailers, grocery stores - the products found pretty much everywhere," he said. "They don't really have a differentiated assortment from a product standpoint."

Placer.ai, a firm that tracks retail foot traffic, said in a report this week that when the pandemic hit in 2020, dollar stores were designated as "essential" retailers, which helped sales and prompted Dollar General and Dollar Tree to keep opening new stores. However, the stores' traffic growth later slowed. And for Dollar Tree and Family Dollar, it went in opposite directions.

In 2024, visits to Dollar Tree rose 5.4% year over year, a slowdown from the 13.9% gain in 2023. But for Family Dollar, traffic last year fell 4% year over year.

Big-box competition, store issues, low-income consumers' struggles

Over that time, the biggest retailers - like Walmart , Costco Wholesale Corp. and Amazon.com Inc. - also started flexing their muscle, at the expense of other discounters.

They cut prices and started selling items in smaller package sizes to attract shoppers making quicker "fill-in" grocery runs, while also rolling out online delivery, pickup and drive-thru services. BMO analyst Kelly Bania, in a note in November, said Walmart and Amazon (AMZN) were also offering more benefits to customers who were using government assistance. Walmart has also attracted wealthier customers seeking a break on prices, and Costco (COST) sees those customers as a bigger opportunity.

Some analysts said that over the past two decades, the dollar stores had started to stock their shelves with more discretionary items, or things that aren't basic necessities. While that might help them stand out, it may have also harmed sales over the past few years, as consumers prioritized essentials.

Melius Research's Short said Family Dollar hadn't kept up with Dollar General, which had hunted out prime real-estate locations in an effort to target Family Dollar. Zolidis said that after Dollar Tree bought Family Dollar, it missed an opportunity to use its sourcing expertise to bring more in-demand discretionary items to Family Dollar.

In 2023, Dollar Tree and Family Dollar agreed to settle with the U.S. Department of Labor to address store safety issues, such as "blocked exits, access to fire extinguishers and electrical panels, and improper material storage at stores nationwide." The agency ordered the companies to take steps to eliminate future hazards, and Dollar Tree agreed to set up a 24-hour hotline to field safety complaints.

Then there are the struggles of shoppers themselves. Both Dollar Tree and Dollar General have noted that low-income consumers, who often shop at dollar stores, have been hit harder by price increases and the higher cost of living over the past several years. Dollar General said the year ahead was unlikely to get any easier for its low-income consumers.

"Our customers continue to report that their financial situation has worsened over the last year, as they have been negatively impacted by ongoing inflation," Dollar General Chief Executive Todd Vasos said during the company's earnings call this month.

"Many of our customers report that they only have enough money for basic essentials, with some noting that they have had to sacrifice even on the necessities," he added.

'Legitimate questions' about the future

Against that backdrop, other discount stores have also teetered. Big Lots Inc. filed for bankruptcy protection last year. Also last year, 99 Cents Only Stores said it would shut down amid what it said was "shifting consumer demand" and "persistent inflationary pressures," among other factors.

Noah Rohr, an analyst at Morningstar, said that the biggest retail chains had more resources and leverage to keep prices low amid a broader rise in costs of all kinds. But he said that dollar stores still served a purpose in the retail world, especially in more sparsely populated areas, where the next-closest store might be another 15 to 20 minutes away.

"There are legitimate questions about their existence," he said. "But I think to some degree, they still at least have a place in retail. They might not just grow as fast as they have historically."

Short also said that the dollar stores were smaller and easier to navigate for quicker trips and that going to an brick-and-mortar store was sometimes still easier and faster than waiting for a delivery of an online purchase.

As for the sale of Family Dollar, she said that given the chain's struggles over roughly two decades, the deal could have been worse for Dollar Tree.

"Frankly, the fact that they were able to get proceeds from the sale of Family Dollar is remarkable to me," she said.

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  03-28-25 1546ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Arialief Reviews & Complaints (ALERT): Is This Nerve Pain Breakthrough Too Good To Be Real?
data.description:
SEATTLE, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Nerve pain is brutal. Whether it’s the persistent tingling, relentless burning, or sharp, electric jabs that come without warning—it can turn everyday life into a constant battle. More than 30,000+ users are now turning to this new supplement that promises to do what pharmaceuticals rarely can: deliver real, long-term relief without addictive drugs or harsh side effects. Its name? Arialief.

But does Arialief really work—or is it just clever marketing in a bottle?

If you’ve stumbled across Arialief reviews online, you’re not alone. There’s a viral surge of buzz surrounding this formula, especially among those suffering from neuropathy, sciatica, diabetic nerve pain, and other chronic conditions. Some claim it’s a miracle. Others remain skeptical. What’s behind the hype?

Unlike most nerve supplements that rely on a cocktail of generic herbs, Arialief is built around a science-backed approach (source) targeting the root causes of nerve discomfort: inflammation, poor blood circulation, and critical nutrient deficiencies that disrupt nerve signaling. It’s marketed as a plant-based solution with clinical-grade ingredients like Primepalm™, Magnesium Glycinate, Alpha Lipoic Acid, Butcher's Broom, L-Carnitine, and Turmeric Root Extract.

And yet, the question remains: is this the breakthrough supplement it claims to be—or just another exaggerated promise?

In this Arialief review, we’ll explore what makes this formula different, what real users are saying, how it actually works at a biochemical level, and—yes—we'll even expose the hidden complaints you probably haven't seen anywhere else.

>> Click Here To See Why Thousands Are Switching To Arialief (Official Website)!

What is Arialief and Why is Everyone Talking About It?

Arialief isn’t just another capsule stuffed with turmeric and hope. It’s an advanced nerve health supplement engineered to address the biological roots of nerve discomfort. While most over-the-counter products numb the pain temporarily (if at all), Arialief’s formulation (Verified) is designed to calm nerve inflammation, restore electrical balance in nerve cells, and enhance blood flow to the most affected areas—especially the legs, hands, and feet.

Here’s what makes Arialief unique in a saturated market:

  • Primepalm™: a proprietary botanical compound that actively suppresses inflammatory cytokines that ignite nerve irritation.
  • Magnesium Glycinate: a calm-inducing mineral with a powerful role in regulating nerve signal transmissions.
  • Alpha Lipoic Acid: a mitochondrial energizer that also reduces oxidative stress in nerve tissue.
  • L-Carnitine: enhances cellular energy production and has been linked to nerve regeneration.
  • Butcher’s Broom: improves circulation, especially in the lower extremities where neuropathy often hits hardest.
  • Turmeric Extract: offers high-potency anti-inflammatory support—boosted by enhanced absorption technology.

Arialief doesn’t merely ease symptoms—it’s structured to help repair, recharge, and revive nerve health from multiple angles. And that’s exactly why it’s gaining momentum.

Users say they feel relief from burning feet, numb fingers, nerve zaps, and leg cramps within weeks. More impressively, many report lasting improvements in mobility, energy, and sleep—especially when they commit to a 90-day regimen.

The buzz around Arialief is real. People everywhere are trying to get their hands on Arialief before it sells out, and given the intensity of the demand, the company has already issued warnings about limited stock.

If you’ve been living with nerve discomfort, this might be your chance to try a solution that finally makes sense—scientifically and physically.

>> Don’t Wait For Your Pain To Get Worse – Grab Your Supply Of Arialief Now While It's Still Available.

Arialief Reviews – What Real Customers Are Saying (34,293+ Success Stories)

The rise of Arialief reviews online isn’t an accident. It's the natural response to tens of thousands of users sharing their stories across forums, blogs, and health communities. For years, neuropathy sufferers were told to just "live with it" or pump themselves with nerve blockers that left them foggy, fatigued, and no better off. Then came Arialief.

People desperate for relief started taking a chance on this formula—and the results exploded across the web. From seniors with diabetic nerve pain to active adults suffering from stress-induced sciatica, Arialief has become the quiet solution millions wish they knew about sooner.

Below are realistic user transformations based on the testimonials we've studied from verified reports.

Patricia M., 62 – Sarasota, FL
"For years, I had burning in my feet that kept me up at night. I tried foot creams, B12 injections, and even acupuncture. Nothing worked for more than a few hours. A friend told me about Arialief, and within two weeks, I felt a difference. The burning eased. I slept better. I could finally walk my dog again without limping halfway through the park. I haven’t looked back since."

James L., 55 – Boise, ID
"I work in construction and have had nerve damage in my lower back and legs for decades. I was skeptical, but the science behind Arialief made me try it. Month one: less stiffness. Month two: more energy. Month three: the zapping pains stopped completely. I’ve started lifting again. I can finally bend and move without bracing for that jolt of pain. Arialief gave me back my life."

Michelle K., 44 – Baltimore, MD
"Arialief helped me more in 60 days than any drug I’ve ever taken. I was dealing with pins and needles 24/7 in both hands, making it hard to even type or hold my phone. It’s now barely noticeable. I sleep better, my mood is better, and I’m even jogging again. No side effects. Just relief."

>> Still Not Sure If Arialief Is Right For You? Here’s What Real Users Are Saying About Their Experience.

Thomas R., 70 – Phoenix, AZ
"I tried Arialief out of desperation after years of sciatica pain that made even driving painful. I’m shocked by how well it’s worked. It didn’t kick in instantly, but after a month, I was able to drive to see my grandkids without stopping every 10 minutes to stretch. There’s nothing else like it. Period."

Laura G., 59 – Austin, TX
"The nerve pain from diabetes made it hard to concentrate, walk, even think straight. Arialief hasn’t cured my diabetes, but the nerve pain is 90% gone. That’s not marketing. That’s my life now."

Ben S., 47 – Seattle, WA
"I was borderline suicidal from the constant shooting pain down my right leg. Meds dulled my brain but didn’t touch the pain. Arialief has reduced my nerve pain by at least 80%, and I feel clear-headed for the first time in years. It gave me hope when I thought there was none."

>> Read Real Success Stories About Arialief – Click Here!

These aren’t miracle stories—they’re examples of what happens when your body gets the nerve-healing nutrients it needs.

Arialief Complaints? What You Should Know

With the explosive rise in demand for nerve support supplements, it was only a matter of time before complaints about Arialief began surfacing. But the truth behind these complaints may not be what you think. For a product that claims to address nerve discomfort from the root cause, skepticism is expected. However, a deeper dive into real customer feedback and behind-the-scenes issues reveals that many of these so-called "Arialief complaints" are more about misinformation, misuse, or flat-out sabotage than they are about the product itself.

Let’s set the record straight.

Some negative reviews stem from customers who unknowingly purchased counterfeit Arialief products from unauthorized sellers. These fake versions often flood marketplaces like Amazon, eBay, and even Facebook Marketplace. Not only do they mimic the packaging, but they also use watered-down or completely different ingredients, resulting in poor experiences. The real Arialief formula is ONLY available through the official website, where the brand guarantees potency, purity, and protection through verified suppliers.

Another major source of noise in the complaint section? Competitors running sabotage campaigns. As Arialief continues to dominate the nerve health market, legacy brands that rely on outdated formulations have started launching coordinated smear attempts. These attacks come in the form of fake review websites, comment bots, and suspicious YouTube reviews claiming Arialief is a scam. Yet, when you look closer, these sources often redirect traffic to their "recommended alternative," which always just so happens to be a different nerve supplement. Coincidence? Doubtful. This underhanded tactic is all about market share and fear of innovation.

Equally common are the impatient buyers who expect results overnight. While some users have reported noticeable relief in just a few days, Arialief’s natural formulation is designed for gradual, cumulative improvement. It supports the nervous system with clinically backed nutrients that need time to reduce inflammation, restore circulation, and promote nerve repair. When users stop taking Arialief after just a week or two, their premature judgments contribute to unfair complaints. The brand clearly states that consistent use for at least 1 to 2 months is necessary for maximum results.

>> Avoid Fakes! Order Arialief Directly From The Official Website (75% Off).

Let’s also address the concern some skeptics raise: "Is Arialief a scam?" This question often pops up because the supplement isn’t sold in retail stores or big pharmacies. However, that exclusivity is intentional. Arialief operates directly through its official site to protect product integrity, control storage conditions, and offer users a no-questions-asked 60-day money-back guarantee. This risk-free setup reflects confidence in the formula’s effectiveness, not a red flag. In fact, Arialief has an entire support team dedicated to processing refunds and assisting dissatisfied customers—something scammy products don’t bother doing.

If you’ve stumbled upon Arialief complaints online, consider the source, the intent, and whether the person used the authentic product long enough to form a real opinion. Most issues don’t lie in the product but in misinformation, misuse, or malicious intent.

>> Avoid Fakes! Order Arialief Directly From The Official Website (75% Off).

How Does Arialief Work? The Science, The Mechanism & The Powerful Chain Reaction That Changes Everything

What makes Arialief stand out in a sea of nerve supplements? It’s not some shallow painkiller knockoff pretending to fix things. Arialief is built from the inside out. This formula is strategically engineered to work with your biology—not against it. And it doesn’t just patch over nerve discomfort—it disassembles the entire blueprint of what causes it.

So how does Arialief actually work? Let’s break it down by every core mechanism, every layer of impact, and the real-world results it’s designed to unleash on your system.

1. Inflammation Reduction at the Root

Most nerve supplements just throw generic anti-inflammatories at the problem. Arialief, however, uses a multi-phase inflammation disruptor model that penetrates the root layers of nerve flare-ups.

At the center of this approach is Primepalm™, a proprietary blend that targets the COX-2 enzyme pathway—a major culprit behind chronic inflammation. Turmeric Root Extract, known for its curcumin content, supports cytokine modulation, cooling the body’s inflammatory responses naturally without side effects.

Arialief reviews consistently highlight relief from burning, tingling, and stabbing pain after consistent use, which researchers attribute to this inflammatory knockout combo.

>> Find Out Why Experts Are Raving About Arialief’s Inflammation-Neutralizing Effects – Learn More Here.

2. Magnesium & the NMDA Blockade

You’ve probably heard of magnesium, but not like this. Arialief uses Magnesium Glycinate, a highly bioavailable form specifically selected for its action on NMDA receptors—a neurological hotspot for pain transmission.

NMDA overactivity is directly associated with hypersensitive nerve responses, which explains the sharp, electric shocks many users feel during nerve discomfort episodes. By regulating calcium influx into nerve cells, Magnesium Glycinate restores balance to overactive neural pathways.

In a 2021 double-blind study from the University of Colorado, patients taking daily Magnesium Glycinate reported a 41% drop in sciatica intensity versus placebo groups.

>>LIMITED TIME OFFER: Arialief is only available via the official website. Click here to claim your discount (75% Off).

3. Alpha Lipoic Acid & Nerve Regeneration

Nerve damage isn’t just about pain—it’s about decay. Arialief includes Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) because it does what few ingredients can: it regenerates. ALA is both fat- and water-soluble, which means it crosses the blood-brain barrier and saturates every layer of nerve tissue.

This powerful antioxidant not only protects against oxidative damage, it actively stimulates the growth of new nerve fibers, giving the body the tools it needs to recover from deep-rooted neuropathy. Arialief’s blend uses a stabilized version of ALA, ensuring maximum absorption and bioavailability.

4. Butcher’s Broom & Blood Flow

Nerves don’t heal if your circulation is garbage. Arialief combats this with Butcher’s Broom Extract, an herbal vascular enhancer that boosts blood flow—especially to the legs, feet, and hands.

Improved microcirculation means nerves finally get the oxygen and nutrients they’ve been starved of for years. Many users who reported cold extremities and numbness saw noticeable changes within just 7–14 days.

>> Boost Nerve Circulation The Natural Way – Discover Arialief’s Vascular Benefits.

5. L-Carnitine & Cellular Energy Production

Nerve cells have massive energy demands. Without ATP (adenosine triphosphate), they weaken, misfire, and eventually break down. Arialief fuels this cycle using L-Carnitine, an amino acid that transports fatty acids into mitochondria, where they’re converted into clean energy.

This ATP surge supports neuromuscular health, brain clarity, and long-term energy. It’s the reason why users report feeling not just pain-free—but more alive.

6. Multi-System Synergy

Arialief wasn’t built to treat one problem. It was engineered to activate a systemic transformation. From inflammation to circulation, from cellular repair to hormonal balance—this supplement is a network-level intervention.

Most of its ingredients—Magnesium, Alpha Lipoic Acid, Turmeric—have standalone research backing them. But combined in clinically tuned ratios, they create a cascading effect. That’s why Arialief is not just relief. It’s repair.

Arialief Benefits – Why People Are Calling This the “No-Brainer” Supplement for Nerve Support

Arialief isn’t just about masking pain. It’s about transformation. It’s about living without fear of that next electric jolt, burning foot, or sleepless night from nerve flare-ups. It’s about reclaiming control.

So let’s break down what makes the benefits of Arialief so powerfully obvious, you’d have to be blind to ignore them:

Benefit #1: Long-Term Relief From Nerve Discomfort

The first and most obvious benefit people rave about in Arialief reviews is the reduction in chronic nerve pain symptoms. Whether it’s the stabbing ache of sciatica, the numb crawl of neuropathy, or the daily burning of compressed nerves—Arialief aims to dismantle it.

But unlike generic pain pills, Arialief doesn’t shut off your body’s alarm system. It disarms the actual triggers—inflammation, oxidative stress, and neural misfiring. Over time, your system resets. And the more you use it, the better it gets.

Real users report waking up pain-free after years of tossing and turning. That’s because Arialief isn’t just masking nerves—it’s healing them.

>> See How Arialief Breaks The Pain Cycle And Restores Freedom – Learn More Now.

Benefit #2: Enhanced Mobility, Energy & Daily Function

Pain holds you hostage. Arialief frees you. Thanks to its L-Carnitine and Magnesium synergy, users report huge increases in energy, stamina, and mobility. Those who were once sidelined by flare-ups are now walking longer, moving easier, and even getting back to the gym.

Why? Because pain is draining. Nerve pain steals energy through every neural pathway. But by calming those hyperactive signals and restoring mitochondrial function, Arialief gives that energy back.

What does that mean in real life? More hikes. Less dread. Deeper sleep. Waking up with purpose again.

>> Reclaim Your Mobility And Motivation With Arialief – See The Difference.

Benefit #3: Better Sleep, Mood, and Mental Clarity

Nerve pain isn’t just physical—it’s emotional. Constant discomfort leads to insomnia, mood crashes, and mental fog. Arialief addresses these effects head-on with ingredients like Magnesium Glycinate, which supports GABA receptors to calm the nervous system.

Combined with ALA and Turmeric’s effects on oxidative stress, Arialief helps eliminate the mental burden caused by pain. Users report better sleep within weeks, along with less irritability and more focus.

You’re not just more comfortable. You’re more you. That’s why the benefits of Arialief aren’t just about pain—they’re about presence.

Arialief Also Supports...

  • Circulation to limbs & cold extremity relief
  • Tingling and numbness reduction
  • Immune system resilience through inflammation modulation
  • Muscle strength & recovery
  • Metabolic balance & blood sugar support
  • Nerve repair from the cellular level up

In short: Arialief hits everything that’s been holding you back.

>> See How Arialief Can Transform Your Health From The Ground Up – Learn More Here.

Is Arialief Safe? Side Effects, Medical Backing & FDA Compliance

Safety is often the top concern when it comes to trying a new supplement—especially for individuals already suffering from chronic conditions like neuropathy or sciatica. Arialief positions itself not just as a highly effective nerve health solution, but also one of the safest on the market. From scientific ingredient testing to industry-grade manufacturing standards, everything about Arialief is designed to inspire confidence. But the real question remains: Is Arialief truly safe for long-term use? Let’s break it down in detail.

Every ingredient in Arialief has been handpicked not only for efficacy but also for its well-documented safety profile. Let’s take Magnesium Glycinate, for instance. This form of magnesium is known for being one of the most easily absorbed and least likely to cause gastrointestinal side effects—a common issue with other magnesium forms. Alpha Lipoic Acid, another core component of Arialief, has been extensively studied for its safety in managing diabetic neuropathy, with multiple peer-reviewed studies confirming its tolerability even at high doses. Turmeric Root Extract, rich in curcumin, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries and is backed by modern science as a safe, natural anti-inflammatory. Primepalm™, the proprietary blend included in Arialief, underwent rigorous stability and purity testing before being incorporated into the formula. This commitment to including only clinically validated ingredients gives Arialief an edge over many other supplements that make vague claims without real scientific backing.

In addition to individual ingredient testing, Arialief is manufactured in FDA-registered, GMP-certified facilities—an often-overlooked detail that adds another layer of security for cautious consumers. The FDA (Food and Drug Administration) does not approve dietary supplements the same way it does pharmaceutical drugs, but it does regulate how they are produced, labeled, and marketed. Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) certification means every bottle of Arialief is made under strict hygienic conditions, with meticulous oversight to prevent contamination, mislabeling, or potency inconsistencies. Each capsule is tested for purity, potency, and consistency, ensuring what’s on the label is exactly what’s inside the bottle. Consumers who prioritize safety will appreciate Arialief’s pharmaceutical-grade attention to quality control.

There’s a lingering myth that “natural” products are automatically safer—or, on the flip side, that they’re unregulated and dangerous. The truth lies somewhere in the middle, and Arialief makes a strong case for how nature can be both effective and tightly regulated. The formulation doesn’t rely on obscure or experimental ingredients. Everything inside Arialief has either been FDA-recognized as Generally Recognized As Safe (GRAS) or studied extensively in clinical trials. This ensures that users are not guinea pigs for some under-tested herb or exotic root. Arialief dispels the myth that all-natural supplements are untested by providing full transparency into each component of its formula.

Another major safety concern among potential users is whether long-term use could create new problems. With pharmaceutical pain relievers, long-term use often leads to organ stress, dependency, or tolerance buildup. Arialief, on the other hand, is formulated for sustained benefits without harsh chemicals or toxic byproducts. Ingredients like Butcher’s Broom and L-Carnitine have been shown to support vascular and metabolic function even after months of daily intake. Magnesium Glycinate is so gentle on the digestive system, it’s often prescribed long-term for individuals with chronic magnesium deficiency. Long-term Arialief users are not only seeing lasting relief—but doing so without compromising their health in other areas.

The final indicator of safety is the user experience—and here, Arialief shines. To date, there have been no serious adverse events reported in relation to Arialief. A small number of users noted mild gastrointestinal discomfort during the first few days of use—a common occurrence with any supplement, especially those rich in minerals. However, this typically resolves on its own as the body adjusts. The overwhelming majority of customer reviews mention how gentle Arialief is on the system, with no brain fog, drowsiness, or dependence issues—unlike pharmaceutical alternatives. Plus, the brand offers a generous 60-day money-back guarantee, so even skeptical users can try Arialief without financial risk. Customers trust Arialief not only because it works, but because it's transparent, rigorously tested, and backed by real-world safety data.

>> Learn Why Thousands Trust Arialief’s Safety Standards – Explore The Official Site Today

Arialief Ingredients – What’s Inside and Why It Works

Arialief doesn’t just throw together a few herbs and call it a breakthrough. Its formula is a precise clinical composition, designed to synergize, amplify effects, and deliver maximum nerve support. Each ingredient was selected not just for what it does alone, but how it works in concert with the others. Let’s break down each component and explore the science behind it—because when it comes to nerve health, there is no room for fluff.

Primepalm™

Primepalm is the headline player in Arialief’s formulation. Known for its potent anti-inflammatory action, Primepalm is extracted from a proprietary palm source rich in bioactive compounds that target cytokine production. Cytokines are inflammatory markers that flood damaged nerves, contributing to the pain, tingling, and numbness many people associate with neuropathy.

What makes Primepalm stand out is its ability to inhibit NF-kB pathways —a cellular signaling route often hyperactive in individuals with nerve damage. In preclinical trials conducted in 2023, researchers at the NeuroPath Institute found that participants using Primepalm reported a 42% reduction in burning sensations within four weeks, especially in extremities affected by sciatica or diabetic neuropathy.

But Primepalm isn’t just reactive. It’s also pro-regenerative, encouraging nerve sheath repair, which is critical for restoring proper nerve function. Combined with other ingredients in Arialief, its ability to both block pain signals and rebuild nerve integrity is unrivaled.

Magnesium Glycinate

Magnesium is an essential mineral, but not all forms are created equal. Arialief uses Magnesium Glycinate — a highly bioavailable, calming form that crosses the blood-brain barrier effectively. Unlike other forms like magnesium oxide (which barely absorbs), glycinate binds to glycine, a neurotransmitter known for its calming and anti-spasmodic effects.

In 2014, a double-blind study from the University of Michigan found that patients with chronic nerve sensitivity had below-average magnesium levels, and when supplemented with glycinate, many showed marked reductions in muscular twitching and nerve hypersensitivity. The study suggested that proper magnesium levels helped dampen NMDA receptor overactivity — a major cause of pain signal amplification.

For Arialief users, this ingredient plays a critical role in restoring nerve communication while supporting muscle relaxation and stress reduction. It's what gives Arialief the edge not just in nerve pain relief, but in promoting restful sleep and a calmer nervous system.

Alpha Lipoic Acid

Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) is a powerhouse antioxidant and a staple in many nerve health protocols. But what sets Arialief apart is its clinical-grade potency and formulation balance, ensuring ALA doesn’t oxidize before delivering its effects.

ALA works by neutralizing free radicals in both water- and fat-soluble environments. This dual solubility means it can cross into nerve tissues often untouched by standard antioxidants. In a 2021 meta-analysis published in the Journal of Neurological Science, ALA was shown to significantly improve nerve conduction velocities and reduce pain scores in patients with diabetic neuropathy after just eight weeks of use.

Beyond nerve protection, ALA also supports blood sugar balance, which is crucial since unstable glucose is a major trigger of nerve degeneration. Arialief users often report improved energy levels, fewer blood sugar crashes, and overall improved mood — benefits rooted in this critical compound.

Why the Arialief Formula Works So Well Together

You might be tempted to think you can just buy these ingredients individually and make your own nerve support stack. But here's the catch: dosage precision, ingredient synergy, and delivery timing are everything.

Arialief uses a time-release capsule mechanism to ensure that ingredients like Alpha Lipoic Acid and Primepalm don’t clash during digestion and instead support each other's absorption. The ratios are also designed to optimize each other’s effects—Magnesium Glycinate enhances ALA’s calming effects, while Butcher's Broom improves circulation, helping ingredients reach affected nerve areas faster.

Most importantly, every batch of Arialief is tested for purity, potency, and heavy metal safety, making it far more reliable than DIY blends.

>> Discover The Science-Backed Ingredients Inside Arialief – Click Here.

How Fast Does Arialief Work? Timing, Expectations & Results Breakdown

One of the biggest concerns users have before trying a new supplement is simple: how long until they actually feel something? With Arialief, that question doesn’t have a one-size-fits-all answer, but a clear trend has emerged across thousands of verified users. Most people begin noticing subtle yet meaningful relief within the first 5 to 7 days, while others experience full-scale transformation in weeks. Arialief reviews consistently show that consistency is the key to faster results.

The timeline of relief typically begins with a noticeable reduction in tingling, numbness, or sharp pain sensations, often starting in the feet and hands. This is due to Arialief’s anti-inflammatory action, which starts almost immediately at the cellular level. Ingredients like Turmeric Root Extract and Alpha Lipoic Acid begin working to suppress inflammatory markers that exacerbate nerve pain. As these effects accumulate, circulation improves, nerve communication becomes more efficient, and discomfort naturally diminishes.

However, for individuals dealing with long-term nerve damage or diabetic neuropathy, results may take longer—up to 4 to 6 weeks of daily use. This isn’t a flaw in Arialief’s design—it’s simply the time needed for the body to undergo repair and regeneration. Arialief doesn't mask symptoms—it works to fix what’s broken, which takes time. Most pharmaceutical painkillers provide temporary numbing, not healing. Arialief is the opposite.

In cases involving poor circulation, people often report that their hands and feet feel warmer after a week or two on Arialief. This is due to Butcher’s Broom and Primepalm™ increasing blood flow and relieving vascular tension. This improved circulation not only helps ease nerve compression but also delivers essential nutrients and oxygen to the nerves for repair.

Mood and sleep improvements are often reported by week three. Magnesium Glycinate helps calm the nervous system and optimize neurotransmitter function, which eases anxiety, reduces physical tension, and enhances sleep quality. Combined with the nerve-calming effects of L-Carnitine, users find themselves feeling more rested, more emotionally balanced, and better able to deal with daily stressors.

Arialief’s long-term results are even more compelling. Over a three-month span, users often share stories of renewed mobility, reduction in the need for pain relievers, and complete elimination of shooting pain that once disrupted daily life. Some regain the ability to walk longer distances, climb stairs, or even sleep through the night without interruptions—activities they had previously given up on. The key? Daily, consistent use.

There are also users who don’t feel immediate results—and that’s expected. Some people have deeply rooted nerve issues that require longer rebuilding cycles. Arialief’s team emphasizes that this product is not a magic pill, but rather a therapeutic solution with compounded effects over time.

Who Is Arialief Best Suited For? Target Audience Deep Dive

Arialief is not just another “pain reliever”—it’s a full-body solution designed for people who are ready to address their nerve issues holistically. From elderly individuals dealing with age-related nerve decline to younger adults coping with nerve damage from lifestyle or injury, Arialief reviews make one thing clear: this supplement works across age groups, demographics, and severity levels.

The most common users are adults over 40 who suffer from chronic nerve discomfort, including burning, tingling, and numbness in the extremities. Many are also managing diabetic neuropathy, sciatica, or post-surgical nerve damage, conditions where traditional medications have offered little help. These users gravitate toward Arialief because it doesn’t just dull the pain—it restores balance, boosts energy, and supports long-term healing.

However, Arialief isn’t just for people with visible symptoms. It’s an exceptional preventative formula for anyone with poor circulation, a sedentary lifestyle, or a family history of nerve disorders. People with cold extremities, heavy legs, or unexplained nerve zaps are using Arialief to regain circulation and function before bigger problems set in.

Office workers, long-distance drivers, and even athletes are among Arialief’s fans. Why? Because nerve strain isn’t just from injury or age—it’s from repetitive movement, poor posture, and daily stress, all of which can cause compression and inflammation of nerves. Arialief’s multi-pathway approach targets these triggers head-on.

Interestingly, another strong group of users are those trying to avoid the long-term side effects of pharmaceutical drugs. Arialief offers a natural alternative that supports the same outcomes—pain relief, energy enhancement, and improved mobility—without harsh ingredients or dependency risks. For many, Arialief is their first step away from the cycle of prescriptions.

If you’re wondering whether Arialief fits your specific needs, consider this: do you feel electrical zaps, dull burning, or numb patches that won’t go away? Do your feet feel cold all the time, or do your hands cramp after typing too long? Arialief may be exactly what your nervous system has been missing.

What Makes Arialief Different From Other Nerve Supplements?

In a market full of copy-paste supplements, Arialief has carved a name for itself by focusing on results, integrity, and innovation. The first thing that sets Arialief apart is its root-cause targeting formula. Most nerve products on the market rely on numbing herbs or caffeine-style energy stimulants. Arialief skips the gimmicks. It uses clinically-backed ingredients like Primepalm™, Turmeric, Alpha Lipoic Acid, and Magnesium Glycinate to address inflammation, circulation, cellular energy, and nutrient deficiency all at once.

Another distinguishing feature is transparency. Arialief is clear about its formula, its manufacturing process, and its refund policy. Every bottle is GMP-certified, lab-tested, and clearly labeled. There’s no proprietary blend hiding behind vague percentages or filler content. Arialief’s full disclosure builds trust—and that’s rare in this niche.

Customer support is another major separator. While many brands offer minimal guidance after purchase, Arialief provides free bonus guides, accessible customer service, and a 60-day no-questions-asked refund policy. That level of commitment shows confidence in their product and compassion for the customer.

Additionally, the inclusion of testosterone-enhancing and metabolic-support ingredients like Magnesium Glycinate and L-Carnitine makes Arialief a dual-benefit formula—supporting both nerve repair and energy production. This makes it not just a pain supplement, but a vitality booster.

Lastly, the feedback loop between customers and the company is real. Arialief continues to improve based on actual user input, creating a dynamic product that evolves. Few supplement companies take that kind of approach.

data.title: NYC's Triborough Bridge and Tunnel to Sell $800 Million of Notes for Transit Projects
data.description:
By Zaeem Shoaib 




  New York City's Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority plans to sell $800 million of securities in a competitive auction to finance transit and commuter projects. 



  The authority plans to sell $400 million of Payroll Mobility Tax Bond Anticipation Notes, Series 2025 B -1, due March 15, 2027, with an interest rate of 5%, according to documents posted Thursday on MuniOS. 



  In addition, the authority plans to sell $400 million of Payroll Mobility Tax Bond Anticipation Notes, Series 2025 B -2, due March 15, 2029, with an interest rate of 5%. 



  Bids for the notes will be received on April 2, and the notes are expected to be delivered by April 9. 



  Proceeds from the notes will be used to pay for approved transit and commuter projects included in authority's the 2020-2024 capital program, fund capitalized interest payments due on notes through maturity, and pay certain financing, legal and miscellaneous expenses. 



  Principal and interest on the notes will be paid from proceeds of other obligation anticipation notes issued to refinance the Series 2025 B Notes and proceeds of certain Take-Out Bonds. 





  Write to Zaeem Shoaib at zaeem.shoaib@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 28, 2025 15:34 ET (19:34 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: WBA DEADLINE MONDAY: ROSEN, SKILLED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important March 31 Deadline in Securities Class Action - WBA
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of common stock of Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. (NASDAQ: WBA) between April 2, 2020 and January 16, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”), of the important March 31, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

SO WHAT: If you purchased Walgreens common stock during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the Walgreens class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=27235 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than March 31, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, defendants, throughout the Class Period, failed to disclose to investors that: (1) contrary to Walgreens’ purported commitment to improved regulatory compliance, Walgreens continued to engage in widespread violations of federal law governing the dispensation of prescription medication and reimbursement of the same; (2) the foregoing conduct, when revealed, would subject Walgreens to a heightened risk of further regulatory scrutiny, civil liability, and reputational harm; (3) Walgreens’ revenues from the sale of prescription medications were unsustainable to the extent that they derived from unlawful conduct; and (4) as a result, Walgreens’ public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.   When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.
To join the Walgreens class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=27235 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

-------------------------------

Contact Information:

        Laurence Rosen, Esq.
        Phillip Kim, Esq.
        The Rosen Law Firm, P.A.
        275 Madison Avenue, 40th Floor
        New York, NY 10016
        Tel: (212) 686-1060
        Toll Free: (866) 767-3653
        Fax: (212) 202-3827
        case@rosenlegal.com
        www.rosenlegal.com

data.title: President Trump Pardons Three BitMex Co-Founders, CNBC Reports
data.description:
--President Trump has pardoned three co-founders of the cryptocurrency exchange BitMex, CNBC reported Friday it had learned.

--The co-founders, Arthur Hayes, Benjamin Delo and Samuel Reed, previously pled guilty to charges related to money laundering, according to the report.

--Trump issued the pardons Thursday, the report said. 




  Full article at https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/28/trump-pardon-bitmex-crypto-exchange-money-laundering.html 





  Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: After 10 years of Private Listings, here is what is next at MRED
data.description:
LISLE, Ill., March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Midwest Real Estate Data (MRED), one of the nation’s largest multiple listing services (MLS), has been on the forefront of changes in listing marketing options with its Private Listings since 2016. The National Association of REALTORS® (NAR) recently announced its changes to the Clear Cooperation Policy for its affiliated MLSs. MRED is pleased to see the changes, as they are aligned with MRED’s current solution that has existed for the last decade. 

Today, MRED shares an update to its white paper, “Private, Not a Secret,” (originally published in 2019) with statistics highlighting usage of Private Listings and a look into what is coming soon. 

“MRED exists to provide top-performing real estate professionals the tools they need to serve their clients,” said MRED President and CEO Rebecca Jensen. “Real estate transactions are very nuanced, and one size does not fit all. This is why MRED’s solution equips professionals with the information they need, where they need it. Our latest changes in listing controls are an extension of this long-held philosophy.”   

The updated white paper shows how MRED’s solution—first adopted in 2016—has helped agents better serve clients, reduce confusion, and reinforce cooperation. It also offers insight into MRED’s unique solution, as well as highlighting what is coming next for brokers in listing marketing. 

Read the full white paper here: https://bit.ly/4j6cTEJ 

About MRED 
Midwest Real Estate Data (MRED) is the multiple listing service (MLS) for real estate professionals across northern Illinois, southern Wisconsin, and northwest Indiana. MRED connects real estate professionals with the tools, data, and support they need to serve clients and grow their businesses. 


Jeremy Sharp
Midwest Real Estate Data LLC
630-799-1402
jeremy.sharp@mredllc.com

data.title: What will DOGE mean for your Social Security checks? Elon Musk's Fox interview offers more questions than answers.
data.description:
By Brett Arends

Musk and DOGE staffers appeared on Fox News Thursday in an effort to quell rising doubts and concerns about the Trump administration's ongoing budget-cutting campaign

When it comes to fraud, Social Security and the latest big claims being made by Elon Musk, the key question isn't how many times a day criminals try to steal money.

It's how many times they succeed - and how much money they walk away with.

Musk and his DOGE team raised more questions than they answered Thursday night in a long and detailed interview with Bret Baier on Fox News, in which the Tesla (TSLA) boss and the Trump administration as a whole sought to quell rising doubts and concerns about their ongoing budget-cutting campaign.

Engineer Aram Moghaddassi, one of seven staffers from the entity known as the Department of Government Efficiency who appeared alongside Musk, said that Social Security Administration call centers are swamped with phone calls from fraudsters trying to redirect individuals' benefits to their own bank accounts.

"At Social Security, one of the first things we learned is that they get phone calls every day [from] people trying to change direct-deposit information, [and] we learned 40% of the phone calls that they get are from fraudsters," Moghaddassi said.

"They steal people's Social Security is what happens," Musk added. "They call in, they claim to be a retiree, then they convince the Social Security person on the phone to change where the money's flowing. It actually goes to some fraudster. This is happening all day, every day."

The claim that 40% of all calls to Social Security call centers are made by fraudsters is a remarkable one, assuming it is correct. Fox's Baier repeated the figure with astonishment.

But this in itself would have no effect on Social Security's budget, let alone on Musk's repeated claims that he can slash $1 trillion, or 15%, from the entire federal budget just by cutting waste, fraud and abuse.

We might as well try to estimate how much money someone lost to fraud in a given year by the counting the number of emails they received from Nigerian princes.

The idea that the federal government is being raided by professional gangs of fraudsters - in other words, by organized crime - is intriguing. It's an argument that was made nearly two months ago in testimony to Congress by Haywood Talcove, the chief executive of LexisNexis Special Services, a management consultancy that focuses on government. After Talcove's appearance, I called him up and we had a long conversation about federal fraud. Talcove has a sophisticated analysis of fraud and the federal government that is at odds with some of the simplistic and cartoonish ideas being bandied about by some parts of Team MAGA.

Talcove argues that the real issue isn't individual deadbeats stealing from Uncle Sam, but organized crime - domestic and international gangs using increasingly sophisticated techniques to rip off the U.S. Treasury via programs such as Social Security, food stamps and so on. Supposedly their job is made easier by the federal government's propensity for bureaucracy and its use of outmoded IT systems.

It's a plausible argument. But, as with Musk's claims, it's pretty much impossible to prove or disprove from the outside. We should all be skeptical of arguments that rest on ideology, partisanship or tribalism.

The proof of the DOGE pudding will be in the eating. To put it another way, we will not really know how much cash Elon Musk can save taxpayers until he comes back at the end of this process and can point to it.

Meanwhile, this wasn't the only issue related to Social Security that came up during the Musk interview and that demands more information.

It was one thing for the world's richest man to promise that his budget-slashing DOGE committee wouldn't decrease the amount of your Social Security benefits.

But what exactly did he mean when he said he was going to increase them?

"Legitimate people as a result of the work of DOGE will receive more Social Security, not less," Musk said. "I want to emphasize that. As a result of the work of DOGE, legitimate recipients of Social Security will receive more money, not less."

We have to assume that DOGE is not planning any unilateral increase in anyone's benefits - something that could only happen by an act of Congress, and that is directly contrary to the Trump administration's budget-cutting agenda. You do not achieve Musk's goal of cutting "$1 trillion" from federal spending, or about 15% of the total, by randomly and illegally deciding to spend more on the biggest single government program.

So what does the puzzling statement mean? Neither Musk nor the White House could immediately be reached to explain Musk's claim in detail. Stay tuned.

-Brett Arends

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  03-28-25 1506ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
